Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic-range protein loss and impaired glucose handling. Elevated liver enzymes and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support systemic involvement. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease, possible diabetic ketoacidosis, and liver dysfunction is recommended.
